channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: These 5 clever mapping upstarts do what Google Maps can’t
item.description: Look, Google Maps is fine. It’s arguably even great—probably one of the most-opened apps on lots of our phones and absolutely one of the most indispensable.
item.dc:creator: JR Raphael, Fast Company

item.title: Hungarian Scientists' Swarming Drones Revolutionize Unmanned Flight
item.description: Hungary's Eötvös Loránd University researchers develop a swarm of 100 autonomous drones that navigate through dense traffic without pre-programming or centralized control.
item.dc:creator: Justin Spike, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: In potential reversal, European authorities say AI can indeed use personal data — without consent — for training
item.description: The European Data Protection Board (EDPB) issued a wide-ranging report on Wednesday exploring the many complexities and intricacies of modern AI model development.
item.dc:creator: csoonline

item.title: YouTube’s top trends for 2024 include Bay Area creators
item.description: Online video giant YouTube has published its year-end top trends lists for 2024 and in includes Bay Area content creators.
item.dc:creator: Pueng Vongs, The Mercury News

item.title: Tabnine code assistant now flags unlicensed code
item.description: Looking to minimize IP liability for generative AI output, Tabnine’s AI coding assistant now checks code for licensing restrictions.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: US effort to curb China's and Russia's access to advanced computer chips 'inadequate,' report finds
item.description: The U.S. Commerce Department’s efforts to curb China’s and Russia’s access to American-made advanced computer chips have been “inadequate” and will need more funding to stymie their ability to manufacture advanced weapons.
item.dc:creator: Aamer Madhani And Josh Boak, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Microsoft’s PC accessories are back, this time with a stylish helping hand from Incase
item.description: Microsoft partners with Incase to relaunch classic PC accessories like mice and keyboards, blending trusted designs with stylish branding and affordability.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence
item.description: The National Defense Authorization Act for 2025 has extensive language on ensuring competition in AI. What can we expect from the Trump administration? The post How the Trump administration is likely to deal with artificial intelligence first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: Musk and Trump are viewed roughly the same by Americans, an AP-NORC poll finds
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Elon Musk, clad in tuxedo and black tie, took the stage at President-elect Donald Trump's Mar-a-Lago resort shortly after the election with all the swagger of the winning candidate himself. “The public has given us a mandate that could not be more clear, the clearest mandate.
item.dc:creator: THOMAS BEAUMONT, SARAH PARVINI and AMELIA THOMSON-DEVEAUX, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The teaser for the actual Superman movie trailer is here, and James Gunn's first DCU movie will make you believe that a man can fly
item.description: Ahead of the Superman movie trailer's official debut on December 19, a 30-second video has teased what to expect from the DCU film.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Barcelona subway recycles energy from braking into power to charge electric cars
item.description: BARCELONA, Spain (AP) — The Barcelona subway grinds to a halt, the doors slide open and commuters pour out to go about their daily business. Little do they know that as they do so, a burst of energy is sent up to street level to help charge an electric car.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH WILSON, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: The push to upskill the technology workforce in federal agencies
item.description: The federal government is facing a massive tech talent shortage, particularly as the development and use of AI continues to gain traction. The post The push to upskill the technology workforce in federal agencies first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: Cupertino: Iconic Flint Center undergoes demolition five years after closing
item.description: The theater where Steve Jobs unveiled the first Macintosh computer in 1984 is reduced to rubble. The Foothill-De Anza Community College District has plans the site.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Lam, The Mercury News

item.title: TikTok asks the Supreme Court to block the U.S. ban
item.description: TikTok on Monday asked the Supreme Court to step in on an emergency basis to block the federal law that would ban the popular platform in the United States unless its China-based parent company agreed to sell it.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fast Company

item.title: DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications
item.description: New realities of emergency work for emergency responders has necessitated increased use of data transmission of live incident video and file sharing. The post DHS Science and Technology Directorate looks to the evolution of first responder communications first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Michele Sandiford, Federal News Network

item.title: House task force releases sweeping end-of-year report on AI
item.description: The House Task Force on Artificial Intelligence (AI) released its sweeping end-of-year report Tuesday, laying out a roadmap for Congress as it crafts policy surrounding the advancing technology. The 253-page report takes a deep dive into how the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: EU investigates TikTok over Romanian presidential election safeguards
item.description: LONDON (AP) — European Union regulators said Tuesday they're investigating whether TikTok breached the bloc's digital rulebook by failing to deal with risks to Romania's presidential election, which has been thrown into turmoil over allegations of electoral violations and Russian meddling.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: 5 reasons why 2025 will be the year of OpenTelemetry
item.description: Some open-source projects are spectacularly successful and become standard components of the IT infrastructure. Take Linux or Kubernetes, for example.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Senate report alleges Amazon rejected warehouse safety recommendations due to productivity concerns
item.description: At least two internal Amazon studies found a link between how quickly the online retailer's warehouse workers perform tasks and workplace injuries, but the company rejected many safety recommendations out of concern the proposed changes might reduce productivity, according to a U.S.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: CompTIA introduces foundational tech certification
item.description: CompTIA has created a certification designed to provide individuals with broad foundational and digital skills that can be used to build a career in technology.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: 10 quantum computing milestones of 2024
item.description: There was a time, back in the early days of computing, when we had vacuum tubes, relays, mechanical gears plus several different types of analog computers. We’re kind of in that same era today with quantum computers.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: How to delete multiple emails at once on Android
item.description: Cut through all the digital clutter and delete multiple emails from your Android simultaneously. Kurt the CyberGuy explains how it's done.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Court denies TikTok's request to halt enforcement of potential US ban until Supreme Court review
item.description: A federal appeals court on Friday left in place a mid-January deadline in a federal law requiring TikTok to be sold or face a ban in the United States, rejecting a request made by the company to halt enforcement until the Supreme Court reviews its challenge of the statute.
item.dc:creator: HALELUYA HADERO, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: NotebookLM Plus is now available to Google Workspace customers
item.description: A new premium version of Google’s NotebookLM AI assistant is now available to Google Workspace customers. Google unveiled NotebookLM last year, initially under the name Project Tailwind, and began testing the AI-powered “notebook” with select users.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Sophia, a famous robot and global icon of AI, wins hearts at Zimbabwe's innovation fair
item.description: HARARE, Zimbabwe (AP) — From answering questions from Cabinet ministers, academics and students on climate change, substance abuse and the law to children’s inquiries about her “birth” and links to God and being described as a talkative feminist, Sophia, the world-famous robot won hearts at an innov
item.dc:creator: BY FARAI MUTSAKA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Metaphor: ReFantazio, Dragon Age, Astro Bot and an indie wave lead the top AP video games of 2024
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — It’s been a rough year in the world of video games, which reeled from some high-profile flops (sorry, Suicide Squad and Star Wars fans) and a relentless series of developer layoffs. Fortunately, indie designers stepped up to fill in the gaps.
item.dc:creator: LOU KESTEN, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: DoorDash steps up driver ID checks after traffic safety complaints
item.description: DoorDash will require its drivers to verify their identities more often as part of a larger effort to crack down on unauthorized account sharing. DoorDash has been under pressure to ensure its drivers are operating legally.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: AMD data center chips vulnerable to revealing data through ‘BadRAM’ attack
item.description: AMD’s Secure Encrypted Virtualization (SEV), meant to protect processor memory from prying eyes in virtual machine (VM) environments, can be tricked into giving access to its encrypted memory contents using a test rig costing less than $10, researchers have revealed.
item.dc:creator: csoonline
